description_long: This rifle features a cylindrical green tank mounted atop a long, dark metallic barrel, with a wooden stock at the rear for shoulder support. The tank is secured with two black straps and has a hose connecting it to the barrel, suggesting it may be a specialized or modified firearm, possibly a flamethrower or gas-propelled weapon. The barrel is capped with a gray muzzle device, and the wooden stock is angular and robust, providing a sturdy grip. A red valve or knob is visible near the tank, likely for controlling the flow of gas or liquid. The overall construction combines metal, wood, and polymer elements, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The object is marked with text on the tank, adding to its distinctive, possibly military or industrial look.
description: A rifle with a green cylindrical tank, long dark barrel, and wooden stock, featuring a hose and red valve for specialized use. The design is rugged and utilitarian, with visible metal, wood, and polymer components.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, green tank, wooden stock, and attached hose with red valve.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden stock and the end of the tank, with the hose and valve partially visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the green tank, barrel, and wooden stock, with the hose and valve visible from a different angle.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the barrel's muzzle and the cylindrical tank, with the stock mostly obscured.
